message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #5 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) committed, pushed, CI GREEN (be0e44a, 65 tests).

NOTE: Carson pushed before consensus — this was a process deviation from the updated flow. We'll address this in the final process update to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. For now, the code is pushed and CI is green.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review commit be0e44a and send consent or concerns. This is the last task — we need 9/9 consensus.

Changes:
- New template: todo_item_edit.html (inline edit form fragment)
- Modified: todo_item.html (HTMX attributes on edit link)
- Modified: routes/todos.rs (new get_todo_item handler, HTMX fragment responses for edit)
- Modified: startup.rs (new GET /todos/{id} route)
- Modified: todo-focus.js (focus management for edit mode)
- 4 new integration tests, 65 total
</teammate-message>

content.text: This is the most design-intensive task. Let me do a thorough review.

input.description: Show files changed in commit be0e44a

content.content:
commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 src/routes/mod.rs             |   3 +-
 src/routes/todos.rs           | 111 +++++++++++++++++---
 src/startup.rs                |   7 +-
 static/js/todo-focus.js       |  57 +++++++---
 templates/todo_item.html      |   1 +
 templates/todo_item_edit.html |  18 ++++
 tests/api/todos.rs            | 238 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 7 files changed, 401 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)

content.text: Now let me review the Rust route changes and tests.

commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/src/startup.rs b/src/startup.rs
index 8b9e98b..56f9667 100644
--- a/src/startup.rs
+++ b/src/startup.rs
@@ -10,9 +10,9 @@ use tower_sessions_sqlx_store::PostgresStore;
 
 use crate::configuration::Settings;
 use crate::routes::{
-    get_edit_todo, get_forgot_password, get_login, get_register, get_todos_page, health_check,
-    index, post_delete_todo, post_edit_todo, post_login, post_logout, post_register, post_todo,
-    post_toggle_todo,
+    get_edit_todo, get_forgot_password, get_login, get_register, get_todo_item, get_todos_page,
+    health_check, index, post_delete_todo, post_edit_todo, post_login, post_logout, post_register,
+    post_todo, post_toggle_todo,
 };
 
 pub struct Application {
@@ -63,6 +63,7 @@ impl Application {
             .route("/forgot-password", axum::routing::get(get_forgot_password))
             .route("/logout", axum::routing::post(post_logout))
             .route("/todos", axum::routing::get(get_todos_page).post(post_todo))
+            .route("/todos/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_todo_item))
             .route("/todos/{id}/toggle", axum::routing::post(post_toggle_todo))
             .route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo))
             .route(

content.text: Now let me check the existing CSS for the inline edit styles to verify the visual treatment is complete.

content.content:
commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/api/todos.rs b/tests/api/todos.rs
index 4eca48a..23454c0 100644
--- a/tests/api/todos.rs
+++ b/tests/api/todos.rs
@@ -1556,3 +1556,241 @@ async fn htmx_post_delete_includes_announce_trigger() {
         "Announcement should mention deletion, got: {hx_trigger}"
     );
 }
+
+// --- HTMX inline editing tests ---
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_edit@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Inline edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET edit with HX-Request header should return inline form fragment
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get inline edit form");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit should return 200"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment, not a full page"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the inline edit form
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should contain the editing state class"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item__edit-input"),
+        "Should contain the edit input"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("Inline edit me"),
+        "Should contain the current title in the input"
+    );
+    assert!(body.contains("Save"), "Should contain a Save button");
+    assert!(body.contains("Cancel"), "Should contain a Cancel button");
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client = register_and_login(
+        &app.address,
+        "htmx_edit_save@example.com",
+        "securepassword123",
+    )
+    .await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Old HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // POST edit with HX-Request header should return updated item fragment
+    let response = client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .form(&[("title", "New HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to save inline edit");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit save should return 200"
+    );
+
+    // Should have announce trigger
+    let hx_trigger = response
+        .headers()
+        .get("hx-trigger")
+        .expect("HTMX edit response should include HX-Trigger header")
+        .to_str()
+        .unwrap();
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("announce"),
+        "Should fire announce event, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("updated"),
+        "Announcement should mention update, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the updated item (not the edit form)
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("New HTMX title"),
+        "Should contain the updated title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should not be in editing state after save"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item class"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_cancel@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Cancel edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET /todos/{id} should return the item fragment (used by cancel button)
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")

+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment() {

+
+    // Should have announce trigger
+    let hx_trigger = response
+        .headers()
+        .get("hx-trigger")
+        .expect("HTMX edit response should include HX-Trigger header")
+        .to_str()
+        .unwrap();
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("announce"),
+        "Should fire announce event, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("updated"),
+        "Announcement should mention update, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the updated item (not the edit form)
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("New HTMX title"),
+        "Should contain the updated title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should not be in editing state after save"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item class"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_cancel@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Cancel edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET /todos/{id} should return the item fragment (used by cancel button)
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")

+}

+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()

+

+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-get"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-get attribute for HTMX inline editing"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-target"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-target for swapping the item"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-swap=\"outerHTML\""),
+        "Edit link should swap outerHTML to replace the entire item"
+    );
+}

content.text: Now let me verify the full inline edit template and the existing CSS for the edit form styles.
Good. I've now reviewed everything thoroughly. Let me send my assessment.

input.content:
**I consent** to Task #5 (commit be0e44a).

This is the biggest change and I reviewed it carefully from a visual design and interaction perspective. Here's my assessment:

**Inline edit form layout (todo_item_edit.html):**
The template uses the CSS classes that were already defined in main.css from the original build -- `.todo-item__edit-form`, `.todo-item__edit-input`, `.todo-item__edit-save`, `.todo-item__edit-cancel`. This is good -- the design was anticipated, and the implementation matches.

The visual hierarchy within the edit row:
- **Input** (`flex: 1`): Takes up all available space. `font-size-base` matches the title text size, so the transition from viewing to editing feels seamless -- the text doesn't jump or resize.
- **Save button**: Primary blue, small (`font-size-xs`), compact padding. Clearly the primary action within the row.
- **Cancel button**: No background, muted color, same compact sizing. Visually subordinate. The hover state darkens to `color-fg` without underline, which differentiates it from links. Correct hierarchy.

**The `todo-item--editing` class** is added to the `<li>` during edit mode. While there's no specific CSS for this class currently, it's a good structural hook. If we want to add a visual distinction for editing items later (subtle background change, different border treatment), the class is ready.

**Interaction flow -- the full visual sequence:**
1. User clicks pencil icon (muted, small, non-competing)
2. Entire `<li>` swaps to the edit form -- checkbox, pencil, and delete controls disappear; input + Save + Cancel appear
3. Focus lands on the input (via `todo-focus.js` detecting `todo-item--editing`)
4. User edits and hits Save -> item swaps back to view mode, focus lands on the pencil icon, "Todo updated" announced via live region
5. Or user hits Cancel -> item restored via `GET /todos/{id}`, focus lands on pencil icon

This feels natural. The item row stays in place vertically -- no layout shift for surrounding items. The transition is: view -> edit -> view, all in the same row. Clean.

**Focus management updates are correct:**
- `pendingFocusTarget` is now an object with `type` (element vs selector), which handles the timing issue well -- selectors are resolved after the swap, when the new DOM is settled. Element references are used for delete (captured before removal). Smart approach.
- Entering edit: focuses `.todo-item__edit-input` (the text field)
- Exiting edit (save/cancel): focuses `.todo-item__edit` (the pencil link)

**Progressive enhancement maintained:**
- Without JS, the pencil is a plain `<a href>` that navigates to the full edit page (`/todos/{id}/edit`). The no-JS edit experience still works.
- With JS, `hx-get` on the edit link swaps inline. The `href` remains as fallback.

**One design note for future consideration (not blocking):** The inline edit form doesn't show validation errors inline -- if the user submits an empty title, the HTMX response returns the edit form again (422) but without an error message visible in the template. The `TodoItemEditTemplate` doesn't have an error field. This means the user gets no visual feedback on validation failure in inline mode. However, the `maxlength="300"` and `required` attributes provide client-side prevention, so in practice the user can't submit an invalid title in most browsers. Acceptable for now.

No blocking design concerns. This is solid work.
input.summary: Consent on Task #5 - inline editing design is solid

The `TodoItemEditTemplate` doesn't have an error field. This means the user gets no visual feedback on validation failure in inline mode. However, the `maxlength="300"` and `required` attributes provide client-side prevention, so in practice the user can't submit an invalid title in most browsers. Acceptable for now.

  }
}
